sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν πιθοίμην μὴ οὐ τάδʼ ἐκμαθεῖν σαφῶς .
sections.translation: I will not hear of not discovering the whole truth .

sections.label: Section 1066
sections.original: καὶ μὴν φρονοῦσά γʼ εὖ τὰ λῷστά σοι λέγω .
sections.translation: Yet I wish you well — I counsel you for the best .

sections.label: Section 1067
sections.original: τὰ λῷστα τοίνυν ταῦτά μʼ ἀλγύνει πάλαι .
sections.translation: These best counsels , then , vex my patience .

sections.label: Section 1068
sections.original: ὦ δύσποτμʼ , εἴθε μήποτε γνοίης ὃς εἶ .
sections.translation: Oh ill-fated man , may you never know who you are !

sections.label: Section 1069
sections.original: ἄξει τις ἐλθὼν δεῦρο τὸν βοτῆρά μοι ;
sections.translation: Go , some one , fetch me the herdsman .

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ταύτην δʼ ἐᾶτε πλουσίῳ χαίρειν γένει .
sections.translation: Leave this woman to glory in her princely stock .

sections.label: Section 1071
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰού , δύστηνε · τοῦτο γάρ σʼ ἔχω
sections.translation: Alas , alas , miserable man — that word alone can I say to you — and no other word ever again . She rushes into the palace .

sections.label: Section 1073
sections.original: τί ποτε βέβηκεν , Οἰδίπους , ὑπʼ ἀγρίας
sections.translation: Why has this woman gone , Oedipus , rushing off in wild grief ? I fear

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: μὴ ʼκ τῆς σιωπῆς τῆσδʼ ἀναρρήξει κακά .
sections.translation: a storm of sorrow will soon break forth from this silence .

sections.label: Section 1076
sections.original: ὁποῖα χρῄζει ῥηγνύτω · τοὐμὸν δʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: Break forth what will ! Be my race ever so lowly , I crave to learn it . That woman perhaps — for she is proud with more than a woman ’ s pride — feels ashamed of my lowly origin . But I , who hold myself son of Fortune

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ἐμαυτὸν παῖδα τῆς Τύχης νέμων
sections.translation: that gives good , will not be dishonored . She is the mother from whom I spring , and the months , my kinsmen , have marked me sometimes lowly , sometimes great . Such being my heritage , never more can I prove

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ποτʼ ἄλλος , ὥστε μὴ ʼκμαθεῖν τοὐμὸν γένος .
sections.translation: false to it , or keep from searching out the secret of my birth .

sections.label: Section 1.1086
sections.original: εἴπερ ἐγὼ μάντις εἰμὶ καὶ κατὰ γνώμαν ἴδρις ,
sections.translation: If I am a seer or wise of heart ,

sections.label: Section 1.1090
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσει τὰν αὔριον
sections.translation: Cithaeron , you will not fail — by heaven , you will not — to know at tomorrow ’ s full moon that Oedipus honors you as native to him , as his nurse , and his mother , and that you are celebrated in our dance and song ,

sections.label: Section 1.1095
sections.original: καὶ χορεύεσθαι πρὸς ἡμῶν , ὡς ἐπὶ ἦρα φέροντα τοῖς ἐμοῖς τυράννοις .
sections.translation: because you are well-pleasing to our prince . O Phoebus to whom we cry , may these things find favor in your sight !

sections.label: Section 1.1098
sections.original: τίς σε , τέκνον , τίς σʼ ἔτικτε τᾶν μακραιώνων ἄρα
sections.translation: Who was it , my son , who of the race whose years are many , that bore you in wedlock with

sections.label: Section 1.1100
sections.original: Πανὸς ὀρεσσιβάτα πατρὸς πελασθεῖσʼ ;
sections.translation: Pan , the mountain-roaming father ? Or was it a bride of Loxias that bore you ? For dear to him are all the upland pastures .

sections.label: Section 1.1105
sections.original: εἴθʼ ὁ Κυλλάνας ἀνάσσων ,
sections.translation: Or perhaps it was Cyllene ’ s lord , or the Bacchants ’ god , dweller on the hill-tops , that received you , a new-born joy , from one of the nymphs of Helicon , with whom he most often sports .

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: εἰ χρή τι κἀμὲ μὴ συναλλάξαντά πω ,
sections.translation: Elders , if it is right for me , who have never met the man , to guess , I think I see the herdsman we have been looking for for a lone time . In his venerable old age he tallies with this stranger ’ s years , and moreover I recognize those who bring him , I think , as servants of mine .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: ἔγνωκʼ ἐμαυτοῦ · τῇ δʼ ἐπιστήμῃ σύ μου
sections.translation: But perhaps you have an advantage in knowledge over me , if you have seen the herdsman before .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: ἔγνωκα γάρ , σάφʼ ἴσθι · Λαΐου γὰρ ἦν
sections.translation: Yes , I know him , be sure . He was in the service of Laius — trusty as any shepherd .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: σὲ πρῶτʼ ἐρωτῶ , τὸν Κορίνθιον ξένον , ἦ τόνδε φράζεις ;
sections.translation: I ask you first , Corinthian stranger , if this is the man you mean .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: τοῦτον , ὅνπερ εἰσορᾷς .
sections.translation: He is , the one you are looking at .

sections.label: Section 1121
sections.original: οὗτος σύ , πρέσβυ , δεῦρό μοι φώνει βλέπων
sections.translation: You , old man — look this way and answer all that I ask — were you once in the service of Laius ?

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: ἦ δοῦλος οὐκ ὠνητός , ἀλλʼ οἴκοι τραφείς .
sections.translation: I was — not a bought slave , but reared in his house .

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: ἔργον μεριμνῶν ποῖον ἢ βίον τινά ;
sections.translation: Employed in what labor , or what way of life ?

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: ποίμναις τὰ πλεῖστα τοῦ βίου συνειπόμην .
sections.translation: For the better part of my life I tended the flocks .

sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original: χώροις μάλιστα πρὸς τίσι ξύναυλος ὤν ;
sections.translation: And what regions did you most frequently haunt ?

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: ἦν μὲν Κιθαιρών , ἦν δὲ πρόσχωρος τόπος .
sections.translation: Sometimes Cithaeron , sometimes the neighboring ground .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρα τόνδʼ οὖν οἶσθα τῇδέ που μαθών ;
sections.translation: Are you aware of ever having seen this man in these parts ?

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δρῶντα ; ποῖον ἄνδρα καὶ λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Doing what ? What man do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: τόνδʼ ὃς πάρεστιν · ἢ ξυναλλάξας τί πω ;
sections.translation: This man here . Have you ever met him before ?

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: οὐχ ὥστε γʼ εἰπεῖν ἐν τάχει μνήμης ἄπο .
sections.translation: Not so that I could speak at once from memory .

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: κοὐδέν γε θαῦμα , δέσποτʼ · ἀλλʼ ἐγὼ σαφῶς
sections.translation: And no wonder , master . But I will bring clear recollection to his ignorance . I am sure he knows well of the time we dwelled in the region of Cithaeron

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: ὁ μὲν διπλοῖσι ποιμνίοις , ἐγὼ δʼ ἑνί ,
sections.translation: for six month periods , from spring to Arcturus , he with two flocks , and I , his comrade , with one . And then for the winter I used to drive my flock to my own fold , and he took his to the fold of Laius .

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: λέγω τι τούτων ἢ οὐ λέγω πεπραγμένον ;
sections.translation: Did any of this happen as I tell it , or did it not ?

sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original: λέγεις ἀληθῆ , καίπερ ἐκ μακροῦ χρόνου .
sections.translation: You speak the truth , though it was long ago .

sections.label: Section 1142
sections.original: φέρʼ εἰπὲ νῦν , τότʼ οἶσθα παῖδά μοί τινα
sections.translation: Come , tell me now : do you remember having given me a boy in those days , to be reared as my own foster-son ?

sections.label: Section 1144
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι ; πρὸς τί τοῦτο τοὔπος ἱστορεῖς ;
sections.translation: What now ? Why do you ask the question ?

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐστίν , ὦ τᾶν , κεῖνος ὃς τότʼ ἦν νέος .
sections.translation: This man , my friend , is he who then was young .

sections.label: Section 1146
sections.original: οὐκ εἰς ὄλεθρον ; οὐ σιωπήσας ἔσει ;
sections.translation: Damn you ! Be silent once and for all !

sections.label: Section 1147
sections.original: ἆ , μὴ κόλαζε , πρέσβυ , τόνδʼ , ἐπεὶ τὰ σὰ
sections.translation: Do not rebuke him , old man . Your words need rebuking more than his .

sections.label: Section 1148
sections.original: δεῖται κολαστοῦ μᾶλλον ἢ τὰ τοῦδʼ ἔπη .
sections.translation: And in what way , most noble master , do I offend ?

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: οὐκ ἐννέπων τὸν παῖδʼ ὃν οὗτος ἱστορεῖ .
sections.translation: In not telling of the boy about whom he asks .

sections.label: Section 1151
sections.original: λέγει γὰρ εἰδὼς οὐδέν , ἀλλʼ ἄλλως πονεῖ .
sections.translation: He speaks without knowledge , but is busy to no purpose .

sections.label: Section 1152
sections.original: σὺ πρὸς χάριν μὲν οὐκ ἐρεῖς , κλαίων δʼ ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: You will not speak with good grace , but will in pain .

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.original: μὴ δῆτα , πρὸς θεῶν , τὸν γέροντά μʼ αἰκίσῃ .
sections.translation: No , in the name of the gods , do not mistreat an old man .

sections.label: Section 1154
sections.original: οὐχ ὡς τάχος τις τοῦδʼ ἀποστρέψει χέρας ;
sections.translation: Someone , quick — tie his hands him this instant !

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: δύστηνος , ἀντὶ τοῦ ; τί προσχρῄζων μαθεῖν ;
sections.translation: Alas , why ? What do you want to learn ?

sections.label: Section 1156
sections.original: τὸν παῖδʼ ἔδωκας τῷδʼ ὃν οὗτος ἱστορεῖ ;
sections.translation: Did you give this man the child about whom he asks ?

sections.label: Section 1157
sections.original: ἔδωκʼ · ὀλέσθαι δʼ ὤφελον τῇδʼ ἡμέρᾳ .
sections.translation: I did . Would that I had perished that day !

sections.label: Section 1158
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰς τόδʼ ἥξεις μὴ λέγων γε τοὔνδικον .
sections.translation: Well , you will come to that , unless you tell the honest truth .

sections.label: Section 1159
sections.original: πολλῷ γε μᾶλλον , ἢν φράσω , διόλλυμαι .
sections.translation: But if I speak I will be destroyed all the more .

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: ἁνὴρ ὅδʼ , ὡς ἔοικεν , ἐς τριβὰς ἐλᾷ .
sections.translation: This man is bent , I think , on more delays .

sections.label: Section 1161
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ ἔγωγʼ , ἀλλʼ εἶπον , ὡς δοίην , πάλαι .
sections.translation: No , no ! I said before that I gave it to him .

sections.label: Section 1162
sections.original: πόθεν λαβών ; οἰκεῖον ἢ ʼξ ἄλλου τινός ;
sections.translation: Where did you get it from ? From your own house , or from another ?

sections.label: Section 1163
sections.original: ἐμὸν μὲν οὐκ ἔγωγʼ , ἐδεξάμην δέ του .
sections.translation: It was not my own : I received it from another .

sections.label: Section 1164
sections.original: τίνος πολιτῶν τῶνδε κἀκ ποίας στέγης ;
sections.translation: From whom of the citizens here ? From what home ?

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς θεῶν , μή , δέσποθʼ , ἱστόρει πλέον .
sections.translation: For the love of the gods , master , ask no more !

sections.label: Section 1166
sections.original: ὄλωλας , εἴ σε ταῦτʼ ἐρήσομαι πάλιν .
sections.translation: You are dead if I have to question you again .
